extraction_note: Three ingestion paths in extract_web.py: (1) DBAASP REST JSON peptide cards; (2) DRAMP general_amps workbook from data/raw/web (Target_Organism `(MIC …)` parsing); (3) CAMPR4/CAMP HTML list + seqDisp detail pages for entries whose Target field contains `(MIC …)`. Dependencies: requests, pandas, openpyxl. Many DRAMP rows overlap primary literature/DBAASP — deduplicate at merge/clean if needed. Per-source caps: max_records and source-specific keys below.

input_pages.robots_notes: Public JSON at https://dbaasp.org/peptides?page=&size=. Throttle ≥1 req/s (script default).
input_pages.selection_reason: Primary structured MIC per peptide–target with medium/inoculum when available.

parser_plan.filter: Monomers only; MIC activity measure; bacterial targets.
parser_plan.notes: verbatim MIC text; canonical units via scripts/utils.py only.

input_pages.access_method: Place DRAMP antibacterial/general Excel export (or curated snapshot matching column names DRAMP_ID, Sequence, Name, Source, Activity, Target_Organism, Pubmed_ID) under local_workbook_path.
input_pages.notes: Substantial overlap with DBAASP/literature is expected — use source_id + sequence + pathogen when deduplicating.

parser_plan.filter: Activity contains Antibacterial; Target_Organism must contain `(MIC …)` clauses parseable by shared regex helpers.

input_pages.license: see CAMPR/R site terms — academic use typical
input_pages.access_method: HTML: harvest seqDisp.php?id=CAMPSQ… IDs from seqDb listings, GET detail pages, parse Activity/Title/Source/Target/FASTA.

parser_plan.filter: Activity contains Antibacterial; Target column must yield ≥1 `(MIC …)` segment; peptide sequence from .fasta table cell.
parser_plan.notes: Many CAMPR entries omit numeric MIC on the HTML card — those are skipped automatically.
